zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Tanner於1525年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾阿斯洛可頓時，她的父親George William Tanner24歲，母親Margaret Tregarthen29歲。她於1547年9月21日與Henry Rayner在英國英格蘭艾可懷特 諾特利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1573年5月11日在英國艾可伯奇享年48歲。

nameMeanings.name: Tanner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern) and Dutch: occupational name for a tanner of skins, Middle English tanner, Middle Dutch taenre. The Middle English form derives from Old English tannere, from Late Latin tannarius, reinforced by Old French taneor, from Late Latin tannator; both Late Latin forms derive from a verb tannare, possibly from a Celtic word for the oak, whose bark was used in the process.
German: topographic name from Middle High German tan ‘woods, pine forest’ for someone who lived near such terrain.

German: habitational name for someone from any of several places called Tanne (in the Harz Mountains, Bavaria, East Prussia, Switzerland) or Tann (Hesse, Bavaria), Thann (Bavaria, Austria, Alsace), Tannen (southern Germany, Switzerland), Thannen (Bavaria).

variants.name: Tenner
meanings: German and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Tanner   2.

English: status name from Old French teneor, teneur, tenor ‘tenant’, for someone who held property from a lord by feudal service, hence an equivalent of Tennant . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Tann
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived in a forest, Middle High German tan. This was originally a distinct word from tanne ‘pine tree’, and denoted a forest of any kind. Inevitably, however, the two became confused, with the result that Tann now denotes only coniferous forests; in this form it is a rather rare and literary word.
English (mainly Norfolk): metonymic occupational name from Middle English tan(ne) ‘oak bark’ (Old French tan), i.e. crushed oak bark used in tanning. See also Tanner .
English (mainly Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Tan, a pet form of Andrew . Compare Tandy .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Tanner was born in 1525, in Aslockton, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, George William Tanner, was 24 and her mother, Margaret Tregarthen, was 29. She married Henry Rayner on 21 September 1547, in White Notley, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She died on 11 May 1573, in Birch, Essex, England, at the age of 48.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Tanner(이)가 1525년 영국 노틴그함실르 아슬록턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George William Tanner(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Tregarthen(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1547년 9월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 휘트 노틀리에서 Henry Rayner(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1573년 5월 11일 영국 에세스 비르치에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Tanner родилась в 1525 г. в местности аслокктон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени George William Tanner было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Tregarthen, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Rayner 21 сентября 1547 г. в местности уит нотли, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она умерла 11 мая 1573 г. в местности бирч, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.
